db-migration.title: Slow query performance on payment processing tables
db-migration.description: Payment API response times increased from 45ms to 380ms during peak hours. Query execution plans show sequential scans on transaction_history table (2.1B rows). PostgreSQL 14 lacks advanced partitioning features needed for optimal performance.
db-migration.resolution: Temporary index added. Long-term fix requires PostgreSQL 16 native partitioning.

db-migration.title: Connection pool exhaustion during Black Friday load
db-migration.description: PgBouncer connection pool saturated at 1000 connections causing 502 errors for 12 minutes. PostgreSQL 14's connection handling lacks improvements available in 16.
db-migration.resolution: Emergency pool size increase to 1500. Need PG16's improved connection multiplexing.

db-migration.title: Replication lag spike causing stale reads in analytics
db-migration.description: Analytics replica DB-PROD-PG-04 showed 850ms replication lag during batch processing window. PostgreSQL 16 logical replication improvements would reduce this to <100ms.
db-migration.resolution: Batch job rescheduled to off-peak. PG16 upgrade needed for permanent fix.

db-migration.title: Stored procedure failure after minor patch
db-migration.description: 3 of 12 legacy stored procedures failed after PostgreSQL 14.8.1 minor patch. Procedures use deprecated syntax that must be rewritten for any future version.
db-migration.resolution: Reverted patch. All 12 stored procedures flagged for rewrite during PG16 migration.

db-migration.title: Security scan flagged PostgreSQL 14 CVE
db-migration.description: Quarterly security scan identified 3 CVEs in PostgreSQL 14.8 with patches only available in 14.10+. PostgreSQL 16 addresses all known CVEs and provides extended support until 2028.
db-migration.resolution: Mitigated with network ACLs. Full remediation requires upgrade to PG16.

security-patch.title: WAF detected Log4j exploit attempts targeting API gateway
security-patch.description: CloudFlare WAF blocked 127 exploit attempts in 24 hours targeting Log4Shell CVE-2021-44228. Attack patterns match known financial services targeting campaigns. All Java-based services potentially vulnerable.
security-patch.resolution: WAF rules blocking attempts. Permanent fix requires patching all 340 Java services to Log4j 2.17.1.

security-patch.title: Vulnerability scan identified 340 services with Log4j 2.14.1
security-patch.description: Qualys scan confirmed CVE-2021-44228 (CVSS 10.0) present in 340 Java microservices, including payment processing, authentication, and data pipeline services. RCE possible on all affected services.
security-patch.resolution: Emergency patching authorized. All services must upgrade to Log4j 2.17.1.

security-patch.title: Kafka cluster running vulnerable Log4j version
security-patch.description: Production Kafka cluster (12 brokers, 450 topics, 2.1M msg/sec) running vulnerable Log4j. Exploitation could compromise message integrity across all dependent services.
security-patch.resolution: Requires Kafka upgrade with Log4j 2.17.1 dependency.

security-patch.title: Elasticsearch clusters vulnerable to Log4Shell
security-patch.description: All 3 Elasticsearch clusters (production 15TB, logging 8TB, metrics 3TB) running vulnerable versions. Total 19 nodes exposed. Exploitation could lead to data exfiltration.
security-patch.resolution: Requires Elasticsearch upgrade to patched version.

security-patch.title: Compliance team flagged SOC2 risk from unpatched vulnerability
security-patch.description: Legal and compliance teams confirm CVSS 10.0 vulnerability with active exploitation constitutes immediate SOC2 Type II and PCI-DSS compliance violation if not remediated within 72 hours.
security-patch.resolution: Board emergency authorization granted. 72-hour remediation window approved.

cost-optimization.title: AWS bill exceeded budget by 40% in November
cost-optimization.description: November AWS spend hit $4.5M against $3.2M budget. Root cause: auto-scaling groups running at maximum capacity 24/7 despite 10x traffic variability. Off-peak utilization averaging 12%.
cost-optimization.resolution: Temporary manual scaling schedule implemented. Need intelligent auto-scaling.

cost-optimization.title: NAT Gateway data transfer costs spiking unexpectedly
cost-optimization.description: NAT Gateway costs reached $180K/month across 3 regions. Analysis shows 60% of traffic is to S3 and DynamoDB which could use VPC endpoints at zero cost.
cost-optimization.resolution: VPC endpoints being evaluated. Requires auto-scaling architecture review.

cost-optimization.title: Reserved Instance coverage dropped to 35%
cost-optimization.description: With static over-provisioning, RI coverage is only 35% of actual capacity. Dynamic auto-scaling would allow better RI matching for baseline capacity, saving an estimated $600K annually.
cost-optimization.resolution: Cannot optimize RIs until scaling patterns are predictable. Requires auto-scaling implementation.

cost-optimization.title: Performance degradation during traffic spike - scaling too slow
cost-optimization.description: Flash sale caused 8x traffic increase. Manual scaling took 15 minutes to respond, causing 3 minutes of degraded service (P99 latency 2.8s vs 200ms target). Auto-scaling needed.
cost-optimization.resolution: Temporary pre-scaling for known events. Need predictive auto-scaling.

cost-optimization.title: CFO escalation: Cloud spend exceeding board-approved budget
cost-optimization.description: CFO formally escalated cloud spend overrun to CTO. YTD spend $36M against $30M budget. Board requires cost reduction plan by Q1. Auto-scaling projected to save $2.1M annually.
cost-optimization.resolution: Auto-scaling implementation approved with $180K budget. 15-month payback period.
